title: Onobrychis humilis
intro: Onobrychis humilis, also known as dwarf field clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, pink to purple flowers and trifoliate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Onobrychis humilis include isoflavones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as in traditional medicine to support women's health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
taxonomy: Onobrychis humilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Onobrychis, which includes other species of wild peas and bean trefoils. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Onobrychis humilis is an annual or perennial herb that produces small, white or pink flowers. The plant is native to the Middle East and Central Asia, and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Its classification is further supported by its characteristics, such as its compact growth habit and small, oval-shaped leaves.
uses: Onobrychis humilis is known to have various medicinal properties, traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its extracts have been studied for potential wound healing and antimicrobial properties. Research suggests that Onobrychis humilis may also have anti-cancer and immunomodulatory effects, although more studies are needed to confirm these findings. Further investigation is necessary to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Onobrychis humilis.
